note: perf v0.3 — tier-1 (fast). B=1, seq_len=16. TRAIN MODE: model.train() set so layerdrop / np.random.uniform path fires. Stochastic duration predictor produces different waveform lengths across runs — correctness must be measured on the common prefix. Compile time is HIGH (58.8s) and recompile_limit=8 is hit — see torch_compilable_check recompiles in import_utils.py:1502.
interpretation: Compiled is only ~11% faster than eager despite 22 graph breaks. The breaks fragment the trace and the HiFi-GAN decoder (with 4 upsample layers) dominates compute, so Inductor's gains on un-broken segments are diluted. Train-mode-only break (np.random.uniform layerdrop at modeling_vits.py:1116) is the BS-106 dominant tag. Discovery strategies should target: (a) the layerdrop np.random.uniform call (rewrite to torch.rand under self.training), (b) find_spec / callable() guards in import_utils.py — these are infra-side and may not be agent-fixable, (c) data-dep F.conv1d guard from output_padding_mask (related to stochastic length prediction).
